title: How Goldman Sachs Prices Variance Swaps
description:

___________________________________________
*📝 Lecture Notes*
https://github.com/romanmichaelpaolucci/Quant-Guild-Library/tree/main/2026%20Video%20Lectures/91.%20How%20Goldman%20Sachs%20Prices%20Variance%20Swaps

**TL;DW Executive Summary**
- A variance swap is a forward contract on annualized realized variance, to price these effectively we need to observe the risk-neutral dynamics and construct an argument for the fair price via arbitrage pricing theory
- To replicate the annualized realized variance, we observe a log transformation of the standard diffusion and applying Ito's Lemma yields a portfolio of market observable (and tradable) instruments
- We can't directly trade a log payoff so we turn to the spanning theorem (Carr-Madan replication) to construct the payoff by an infinite strip of calls and puts revolving around a hinge or pivot point to ensure relative liquidity isolating only out-of-the-money options for practical purposes
- The hinge or pivot point varies but CBOE standardizes it to the first strike below the forward price and discretizes the continuous formula to a set of liquid strikes to produce the VIX
- If there is interest in the future I would love to do a practitioner's guide on variance swaps focusing on the VIX and the discretization

I hope you enjoyed, and I hope you learned something!

- Roman
___________________________________________
*📖 Chapters:*
00:00 - Goldman Sachs Quant Strats Pricing
02:25 - On Variance Swaps
05:57 - Notes Regarding Variance Swaps
08:09 - Objective: Find Implied Variance Strike
08:45 - Underlying Dynamics and Payoff
09:47 - Replicating the Annualized Realized Variance
21:28 - Risk-Neutral Annualized Realized Variance
24:40 - Replicating the Log Payoff with Carr-Madan
37:15 - Risk-Neutral Implied Variance Strike (Volatility Swap Price)
38:50 - Goldman Sachs Desk Note on Variance Swap Price
40:23 - TL;DW Executive Summary
